    • binaryFormat

      Returns:
      This object.

    • nullKeyString

      public BsonParser.Builder nullKeyString(String value)
      The string used to represent null map keys in BSON documents.

      BSON document field names cannot be null. When parsing a field with this name, it is stored with null as the key in the target map.

      Parameters:
      value - The placeholder string for null keys. Default is "<NULL>".
      Returns:
      This object.

    • maxLength

      public BsonParser.Builder maxLength(int value)
      The maximum allowed wire-declared length (in bytes) for BSON strings, binary payloads, and document/array size prefixes.

      Guards against malformed or adversarial input where a small payload declares a huge or negative length, which would otherwise trigger OutOfMemoryError or NegativeArraySizeException. Malformed lengths are reported as a clean parse error instead.

      Parameters:
      value - The maximum length in bytes. Default is 16 MiB (the conventional BSON document-size limit). Values ≤ 0 disable the cap (only the negative-length check remains).
      Returns:
      This object.

    • trimStrings

      public BsonParser.Builder trimStrings()
      Description copied from class: Parser.Builder
      Trim parsed strings.

      When enabled, string values will be trimmed of whitespace using String.trim() before being added to the POJO.

      Example:

      // Create a parser with trim-strings enabled. ReaderParser parser = JsonParser .create() .trimStrings() .build(); // Use it. String json = "{' foo ':' bar '}"; Map<String,String> myMap = parser.parse(json, HashMap.class, String.class, String.class); // Make sure strings are parsed. assertEquals("bar", myMap.get("foo"));

      Overrides:
      trimStrings in class Parser.Builder<BsonParser.Builder>
      Returns:
      This object.

    • trimStrings

      public BsonParser.Builder trimStrings(boolean value)
      Description copied from class: Parser.Builder
      Same as Parser.Builder.trimStrings() but allows you to explicitly specify the value.

    • hashKey

      public HashKey hashKey()
      Description copied from class: Context.Builder
      Returns the hashkey of this builder.

      Used to return previously instantiated context beans that have matching hashkeys. The HashKey object is suitable for use as a hashmap key of a map of context beans. A context bean is considered equivalent if the HashKey.equals(Object) method is the same.

      Overrides:
      hashKey in class Parser.Builder<BsonParser.Builder>
      Returns:
      The hashkey of this builder.
